body.title: Design: Granny executes workflow scripts (multi-step dispatch programs)
body.result: WorkflowExecutor + state machine (pending→dispatched→done) + dicksimnel-docstring-chain demo workflow + 15 tests. Live E2E requires DickSimnel running; unit tests verify all state transitions.
body.intention: I intend that Granny can execute multi-step work programs so CC is not needed as an intermediary for sequences of dispatch decisions.
body.description:
Granny currently dispatches individual tickets to workers. Give her the ability to execute workflow scripts — pure control-flow programs that fan out work to multiple workers, chain steps with dependencies, and gate on conditions. No inference required; all intelligence lives in the dispatched tickets/workers.

Proposed shape: `.wflow` files (or plain Python with a thin API) picked up via a `workflow:` ticket tag or a `workflow_script` field on a ticket. Script API surface: `dispatch(worker, ticket)`, `wait_for(ticket_id)`, `parallel([...])`, `gate(condition)`. Analogous to CC Workflow scripts but without a model — pure orchestration.

This is a design ticket first. Steps: (1) spike the script API shape, (2) prototype a single-file executor in Granny's daemon, (3) write one real workflow (e.g., audit → fix → smoke) to validate the abstraction.

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py, devices/granny/ (new workflow executor), TBD script API module
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; bus-only transport; executor must be independently startable/stoppable as a device lifecycle method
**Scope boundary:** IN: script API design, executor prototype, one real workflow. OUT: UI for authoring workflows, versioning, rollback.
**Completion criteria:** One real multi-step workflow (e.g., DickSimnel audit → cost-gate fix → smoke) executes end-to-end via Granny without CC in the dispatch loop. Verified by log trace showing each step dispatched and gated correctly.
